content.full_text:
Fern Polrrrcar PnacrrcEs ConaurssroN
428 J Street o Suite 620 . Sacramento, CA g5gl4_2329
(916) 322-5660 o Fax (916) 322-0886
May 6,2005
Bonnie Stone, Elections Analyst
City of San Diego
Office of the City ClerVElections
202 C Street, MS 2.A.
San Diego, CA 92101
Your Request for Advice
Our FiIe No. A-05-090
Dear Ms. Stone:
This letteris in response to your request on behalf of the City of San Diego for
advice regarding the campaign provisions of the Political Reform Act lttre ,.Act,,).r
QUESTION
May candidates and committees involved in a special election to be held on July
26,2005, combine the second pre-election campaign statement with the first semi-annual
statement due July 31,2005?
CONCLUSION
In connection with a July 26,2005, special election, candidates and committees
may use a filing schedule that combines the pre-election campaign statement with the
semi-annual statement due on July 31, 2005.
During an election, candidater:::l""r,,r.,ro,ved in the election are
required to file two campaign statements disclosing contributions received and
expenditures made in connection with that election-. These pre-election campaign
statements are generally filed 40 days before the election and again 12 daysiefore the
election. (Section 84200.8.) Semi-annual statements are filed ianuary : iurrO rrrty r t,
respectively. (Section 84200.)
I Govemment Code sections g1000 - gl0l4
18109-18997, ofthe California Code of Regulations.
Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
Re:


File No. 4-05-090
Page No. 2
There is a mechanism in the Act that permits the combining of statements when
an overlapping reporting period occurs, or the filing of the semi-annual statement would
be an additional, unnecessary filing to the trvo pre-election statements. (Sectiong4205.)
In this case, filing the two pre-election statements in connection with a July 26,2005,
election will satisfu the semi-annual filing requirement
A campaign statement filing schedule which outlines the due dates for filing
statements in connection with the election is enclosed for your convenience.
If I may be of any further assistance, please do not hesitate to contact me at (g66)
275-3772.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel
By:
Rindahl
Political Reform Consultant
Technical Assistance Division
Enclosure
TR: rd
